title: Cops in plain clothes wielding lathis during CJP protest: Necessity or ambiguity?
content: Plainclothes police involvement in protests drew criticism for lacking uniforms and identification. Senior officers explained this is necessary for intelligence gathering and undercover operations. Such deployments are not new and aim to manage large crowds effectively. While not strictly prohibited, it creates ambiguity during public demonstrations. Police leadership now discourages this practice unless situations escalate or manpower is short.

title: FBI nabs Harmanveer Singh over Canada-based gang's drug trafficking racket
content: An Indian national, Harmanveer Singh, was arrested in California for alleged drug trafficking.  He is linked to a Canada-based organized crime group led by Ravinder Dhanda.  This arrest is part of a larger international law enforcement operation targeting crime syndicates.  Authorities charged thirty-seven individuals under "Operation Hard Ball" across multiple countries.  Another Indian national, Nitish Kaushal, was also arrested while attempting to cross into Canada.

title: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu lead India's medal hopes at CWG 2026
content: India begins its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow with a compact contingent.  The program features ten sports, a significant reduction from previous games.  Athletics forms the backbone of the campaign with many competitors.  Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain are key figures for India.  Glasgow offers a learning opportunity before India hosts the games in 2030.

title: 'Put your own house in order': India slams Pakistan over crossborder terror at UN
content: India strongly criticized Pakistan's continued use of crossborder terrorism as a state policy.  Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish stated Jammu and Kashmir is an inalienable part of India.  He highlighted Pakistan's illegal occupation as the sole unresolved issue concerning the region.  Crossborder terrorism has eroded the trust needed for bilateral cooperation, India noted.  Pakistan should focus on its domestic challenges instead of targeting India.

title: Johnson & Johnson announces its entry into the US robotic surgery market
content: Johnson & Johnson secured FDA approval for its Ottava robotic surgical system.  This authorization allows entry into the growing soft-tissue robotic surgery market.  The system is approved for several general surgery procedures in the upper abdomen.  Ottava features a smaller operating room footprint compared to existing systems.  The company aims to challenge Intuitive Surgical's dominance in this field.

title: Ram temple trust puts off appointment of CEO and 3 members
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust deferred key appointments and restructured religious oversight.  A nine-member committee, including five seers, will now manage temple responsibilities.  Decisions on the chief executive officer and new trustees were postponed for further review.  The trust expressed dissatisfaction with SBI's donation counting system and replaced staff.  Public faith was not perceived as dented by donation counting controversies.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: US accuses Kimi K3 AI model maker Moonshot AI of 'stealing' Anthropic's Fable model
content: White House official Michael Kratsios has accused Chinese startup Moonshot AI of stealing Anthropic's technology to build Kimi K3, alleging it distilled the Fable model at scale using a covert internal platform. He also said Moonshot accessed banned Nvidia GB300 chips in Thailand. The charge follows Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's sanctions warning over Chinese AI models. Moonshot has not responded to the allegations.

title: Egypt reopens 3,400-year-old tomb of Amenhotep III after 20-year restoration
content: The ancient tomb of Amenhotep III has reopened after a 20-year hiatus, inviting travelers to delve into Egypt's rich history. Set to coincide with the Grand Egyptian Museum's forthcoming launch, the tomb's restoration has revitalized its stunning wall art and hieroglyphs. Once a resting place for the pharaoh and his queens, this site showcases significant ancient beliefs while aiming to attract tourists eager to explore its historical treasures.

title: Ford worker says he was fired over $1.95 cookies despite proof of payment
content: A Ford employee faced termination after an alleged cookie theft from a self-checkout kiosk. Bank records later proved the $1.95 snack had been paid for by the diabetic worker. Ford acknowledged the error and offered the employee back wages and his old job. However, the former electrician declined the offer and is now preparing to sue the automaker. This incident highlights concerns about workplace technology and employee investigation protocols.

title: NYC Mayor Mamdani hires 'tech crew': Here's what they will actually do
content: New York City has opened applications for a new kind of government job. Mayor Zohran Mamdani is hiring software engineers, product designers, user researchers and data experts for Public Interest Technology Crews—around 30 full-time roles across four teams, backed by $5.24 million in the city budget. Their first build is a complaint portal enforcing New York's Click to Cancel subscription rule, live from October 1.

title: Did Russia aid Iranian drone attacks on CIA sites? What report says
content: US intelligence agencies are investigating Russia's potential role in recent Iranian drone attacks.  Analysts are examining the precision and effectiveness of these strikes.  Russia may have provided targeting intelligence and advanced drone technology.  This involvement could mark a significant escalation in undermining US operations.  At least two CIA facilities were targeted in March.

title: Vijay fans burst firecrackers inside packed theatre during 'Jana Nayagan' screening
content: Fans celebrated Vijay's film 'Jana Nayagan' by bursting firecrackers inside a Bengaluru theatre. This unusual celebration drew widespread attention and mixed reactions online from viewers. The incident occurred during the screening of the actor's final film before entering politics. Many moviegoers recorded the moment, which quickly went viral on social media platforms. The event highlighted fan passion and reignited safety discussions within cinema halls.

title: Candace Owens slams Erika Kirk, says Charlie Kirk fueled her fame ambitions
content: Candace Owens intensified her public feud with Erika Kirk, accusing her of pursuing fame and fortune throughout her life. In a podcast, Owens claimed Charlie Kirk was Erika's "ticket to more fame" and argued a recent article criticizing her instead exposed Erika's character. She also questioned Erika's motives, saying her focus had always been on public attention, money, and influence.

title: Blake Lively's new inner circle fuels Taylor Swift friendship buzz
content: Blake Lively has sparked fresh speculation about her friendship with Taylor Swift after sharing photos with a new group of friends following her absence from Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. While fans continue to question their relationship, Lively remains focused on new friendships as her legal dispute with Justin Baldoni continues.

title: Caleb Williams vs Patrick Mahomes: Who had the stronger NFL rookie season?
content: Caleb Williams impressed with a productive rookie season, showing poise and playmaking ability despite difficult circumstances. However, Patrick Mahomes made the greater early impact overall. After spending his rookie year learning, Mahomes exploded in his first season as a starter, winning NFL MVP, throwing for 5,000 yards and 50 touchdowns, and setting a benchmark few young quarterbacks have matched.

title: Imran Khan joins protest: 'I'm here to show support and solidarity'
content: Actor Imran Khan joined a student protest in Mumbai on Wednesday. He expressed solidarity with the young demonstrators participating in a peaceful gathering. Videos of Imran Khan marching amid a downpour are now going viral online. He condemned the reported use of force against student protesters in his statement. Imran Khan had previously posted his strong condemnation of police action.

title: 'I'm sorry': Why Argentina's assistant coach apologised after FIFA World Cup final
content: Argentina assistant coach Roberto Ayala apologized for his actions during a post-match altercation.  He admitted his behavior was unacceptable after Spain defeated Argentina in the World Cup final.  Ayala expressed regret for losing control and stated his intention was to separate players.  He accepted full responsibility for his actions during the chaotic post-game scenes.  FIFA has launched an investigation into the post-match brawl involving players and staff.

title: 'When you fear failure, you play to save yourself': Shreyas Iyer's message to Team India
content: India captain Shreyas Iyer wants players to avoid fear of failure.  He stressed focusing on expressing themselves freely on the field.  This comes after a disappointing white-ball tour of England and Ireland.  The team aims to adapt quickly to new conditions in Zimbabwe.  Iyer believes this challenging phase will make the team stronger.

title: 'It should not be hijacked politically': Salman Khan on students' paper leak protest
content: Bollywood star Salman Khan has voiced his support for students protesting against paper leaks, calling the issue "very serious" and urging that the movement remain focused on education rather than politics. Taking to Instagram, Salman praised students for coming together to demand a better education system and also acknowledged the support extended by their parents. However, he expressed sadness over reports that the otherwise peaceful protests had turned violent.

title: Politics of silence: Is NCP(SP) chief Sharad Pawar keeping options open on Delimitation Bill?
content: The silence of Sharad Pawar's NCP(SP) regarding the Delimitation Bill is stirring political rumors. While Sharad Pawar joined the opposition for demonstrations on Tuesday, he met Prime Minister Narendra Modi the next day. Supriya Sule, his daughter, expressed conditional support for Bill if it proposed seat increases across states evenly. This reflects Pawar's history of maintaining strategic ambiguity, thereby ensuring his party remains adaptable within shifting political currents.

title: SBI PO Admit card 2026 released; Prelims hall tickets available for 1,500 vacancies
content: The State Bank of India has released the SBI PO Admit Card 2026 for candidates appearing in the Preliminary examination for 1,500 Probationary Officer vacancies. Hall tickets were issued on July 22, 2026, and can be downloaded using registration credentials. The Preliminary examination will be held on August 1 and 2, 2026. Candidates must verify all details, carry the required documents and follow the examination day instructions mentioned on the admit card.

title: Oil prices touch six week high as Strait of Hormuz is 'completely closed'
content: Crude oil prices surged as Middle East conflict escalated on Thursday. Brent crude rose above $96 a barrel and WTI climbed past $88. Fresh attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea fueled supply concerns. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed control of the Strait of Hormuz. Houthi naval blockades further amplified worries over global energy shipments.

title: Why is cricket not part of the Commonwealth Games 2026? EXPLAINED
content: Glasgow will host the 2026 Commonwealth Games with a reduced sports program.  Cricket, hockey, and badminton are notably absent from the upcoming event.  This decision impacts India's medal prospects significantly after their strong performance.  Many of India's medal-winning sports from Birmingham 2022 are no longer included.  Organizers cited financial and logistical reasons for the sport reductions.

title: US signs 30-year nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia
content: The United States and Saudi Arabia signed a civil nuclear cooperation agreement. This pact allows for a joint study on uranium enrichment facilities. US officials stated the deal upholds safety and nonproliferation standards. Congress will review the agreement, facing potential opposition from lawmakers. The deal comes amid ongoing military operations against Iran.

title: 'I sent a message to Alexis': Father reveals truth behind Messi's viral pep talks
content: Lionel Messi's viral speech before the World Cup final was tactical.  His teammate's father clarified the captain's words were taken out of context.  Messi urged players to focus on football and their game plan.  This explanation dismisses speculation of internal team issues.  Spain ultimately won the match, proving to be the better side.

title: Curbs on foreign funding not uniquely Indian, says govt ahead of bill to tweak FCRA
content: The government staunchly supported the amendments to the Foreign Contribution Regulation Act (FCRA), arguing that unregulated foreign money poses a threat to democratic integrity. They clarified that the act does not prohibit NGOs from accessing foreign funds, particularly for faith-based initiatives. The restrictions on foreign nationals in crucial positions ensure that all contributions align with verified Indian governance.

title: Candace Owens demands retraction as Charlie Kirk feud intensifies
content: Candace Owens and TPUSA spokesman Andrew Kolvet publicly clashed after Kolvet questioned a Daily Mail report involving Erika Kirk. Owens responded by posting screenshots she said showed the newspaper requested comment before publication. Kolvet accused her of benefiting from the controversy, while Owens challenged TPUSA to seek legal action if the reported quotes were fabricated.

title: Ines Garcia's old comments on Jude Bellingham spark fresh row
content: Old videos of Lamine Yamal's girlfriend, Ines Garcia, have resurfaced online, sparking debate after she chose Jude Bellingham over Yamal and Kylian Mbappe in a casual conversation. Another clip showed Garcia commenting on Yamal's ex, Nicki Nicole. The videos' timing and full context remain unverified.

title: Improving liquidity helping robust growth in credit: RBI
content: India's economy shows resilience amid global uncertainties and fragile geopolitics.  Liquidity conditions have improved, supporting robust credit growth across the nation.  The external sector remains steady, with foreign investment inflows strengthening its outlook.  Rural and urban demand conditions are healthy, reflecting strong economic activity.  Retail inflation edged up in June, driven by food and fuel components.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation is non-negotiable, protest to continue, says CJP
content: Delhi Metro shuts 16 stations, including Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Delhi Gate and Central Secretariat; interchange facility available at Rajiv Chowk, Mandi House and Central Secretariat

title: NCB dismantles transnational drug syndicate, arrests Myanmar-based “kingpin”
content: The accused is wanted in three cases registered by the NCB and is also connected to seven other cases instituted by the police and the DRI

title: Africans, Russians, Ukrainians among 2,089 deported from Assam since 2016
content: The State government tells the Assam Assembly that 61,259 Hindus and Christians were among 1,72,673 foreigners detected in Assam till May 31

title: 19-year-old may lose vision in right eye due to pellet injury, says family
content: Sahil Lochab underwent a surgery to remove pellets; doctors have informed that there is only a “1% chance” of vision returning in the injured eye as the pupil was damaged, his family said

title: 10 more deaths raise Assam flood toll to 41
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the damage is far greater than anticipated and more bodies are being recovered from the affected districts; more rains forecast over next four days

title: PM should apologise to students, Education Minister must resign: Rahul Gandhi
content: The Congress leader says there have been 156 paper leaks and ‘zero convictions’, the Opposition is ‘100% in agreement’ with the protesters’ demands, describing them as ‘legitimate’ and ‘non-negotiable’

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Heavy security deployed at Jantar Mantar, as crowd of protesters swells
content: Rahul Gandhi using students' issues for political mileage, says J.P. Nadda; Wangchuk seeks 'unequivocal assurance' that no young protester will face punitive, retaliatory legal action

title: Jammu and Kashmir government identifies 654 vulnerable villages as wet weather to continue for next 24 hours
content: “As a precautionary measure, 1,505 people from vulnerable areas have already been shifted to designated rescue centres, while evacuation plans remain in place for other vulnerable locations,” says government spokesman

title: NHPC’s own 2024 paper recorded gas fires in Teesta-VI tunnels before fatal blast
content: The July 20 incident is likely to have occurred due to an isolated patch near the face of tunnel 4B, which was sudden and gave no indication or time for the safe evacuation of the people working inside the tunnel, the NHPC says

title: Amid Lok Sabha logjam, Centre offers debate on NEET paper leak, says street protests ‘won’t help’
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister says government is prepared for a “detailed discussion”; Congress seeks adjournment motion to set aside all other business for the debate; demands Education Minister’s resignation

title: Protesters at Jantar Mantar find relief at free clinics set up by doctors
content: The clinic, apart from having paracetamol, painkillers, bottles of Dettol, and sanitary pads, also has a machine to check blood pressure, an oximeter, and thermometers. The medicines and the equipment were donated by the people and the 10-12 doctors working there

title: Delhi HC dismisses PIL over Sonam Wangchuk’s removal from Jantar Mantar
content: “Since an FIR is sought to be lodged in relation to a reportedly solitary incident, it is always open for the petitioner to take recourse to 173 of BNSS,” says court

title: NEET’s promise has failed; it’s time to trust States, writes M.K. Stalin
content: The student protests must serve as a wake-up call — not merely to improve exam security but to fundamentally rethink the entire framework of high-stakes national entrance exams

title: Maharashtra begins rollout of Farmer Loan Waiver Scheme; first 500 beneficiaries announced
content: Lists will be published at the village level in the Gram Panchayat, various executive cooperative society offices and the respective bank branches, says CMO

title: ‘Over 5,200 applications’: Ram Temple trust defers CEO selection by a month
content: In second meeting amid donation theft row, trust announces reconstituted committee of seers to oversee religious affairs of temple; secretary to be appointed on interim basis until CEO is picked

title: BJP, Oppn. take divergent view of police action on protesters
content: Monday’s march was ‘not peaceful’ and its organisers must be held accountable, says Delhi Minister; BJP MP says Centre acted with ‘sensitivity’; AAP highlights instances of women being ‘assaulted’; cadre standing with Rahul: Delhi Cong. chief

title: Diesel generator at textile showroom in Katpadi catches fire
content: The police said that it was around 2.30 p.m. when a few workers in the showroom noticed thick smoke coming from the diesel generator

title: Two-day Thiruvasagam conference to be held in Tiruvannamalai
content: As part of the event, around one lakh participants worldwide will participate, mainly virtually, in writing 10 compositions from Thiruvasagam

title: Meet us here, CJP tells govt. as crowd swells at Jantar Mantar
content: If Ministers want to talk, they should come to people, says CJP; group appeals to Wangchuk to end fast; people turn up in thousands at Jantar Mantar; some of the protesters injured in police action back at venue

title: Doctors in Kerala’s Nilambur save tribal woman, baby after rare uterine rupture
content: The 25-year-old woman from the Cheerakkuzhi tribal hamlet near Chugathara was brought to the Government District Hospital Nilambur hospital with a rare uterine rupture that left the baby lying inside her abdominal cavity

title: City hospital launches integrated robotic surgery programme
content: The initiative brings robotic-assisted surgery across multiple specialties under a common clinical framework

title: Doctor in Kerala’s Kannur booked over alleged medical negligence, hospital denies charges
content: The complainant alleges she was discharged after an endoscopic procedure despite a fish bone remaining lodged in her throat

title: SHITIJ 2.0: nationwide start-up incubation programme inaugurated at ICAR-CPCRI
content: The second edition of SHITIJ is being implemented through a nationwide network of seven hub institutes and their associated spoke institutes

title: Activists, celebrities join Kochi protests in solidarity with Delhi students
content: Writers, filmmakers, actors and activists back agitation over the alleged NEET paper leak and police action in Delhi; solidarity protests held across Kochi, with marches, public meetings and a candlelight vigil drawing students and youngsters; speakers criticise alleged police excesses and call for wider public support for the student movement

title: Kerala UDF constituent IUML trains guns on Revenue Minister A.P. Anil Kumar
content: IUML leaders from the Wandoor constituency accuse Mr. Anil Kumar of sidelining the party after assuming office as Revenue Minister

title: Why so selective on paper leaks, Nadda asks Rahul
content: J.P. Nadda said Rahul Gandhi did not mention incidents of paper leaks and related irregularities reported under governments led by Opposition parties, including the Congress

title: Piracy fails to dampen advance bookings for Chief Minister Vijay’s Jana Nayagan
content: According to data shared by trade tracker Sacnilk, Jana Nayagan has sold 7.37 lakh tickets nationwide, and grossed ₹16.78 crore in advance bookings across 9,566 shows

title: Did not refuse to meet KSU leaders, says Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister’s comments came in response to reports that he had refused to meet KSU president Aloshious Xavier amid controversies over the appointment of an individual with alleged past links to the Students Federation of India  as government pleader

title: SFI demands that UDF government rid the Kerala Governor of Chancellor power
content: Other demands include free bus travel for students, permanent Vice-Chancellors, free education till degree level, political freedom on campuses, permanent Principals in government colleges, regulating fees in private and self-financing institutions, and a unified examination system

title: Madras High Court dismisses Senthilbalaji’s plea to prevent police from ‘harassing’ him and his brother
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan says, no such direction can be issued when the brothers had obtained anticipatory bail in pending cases and it is premature to issue such a direction with respect to future cases

title: Government allays fears over FCRA Bill, clarifies on designated authority
content: PIB says the Bill won’t alter religious character of places of worship attached by designated authority

title: Body of Uttar Pradesh native fished out of Vilangad river
content: The body of Nigam Kumar was recovered from Vilangad river after a four-day search with the support of various rescue teams

title: Lawyers back protesters, urge PM to order probe on police crackdown
content: Supreme Court Bar Association adopted a resolution seeking an immediate, impartial and time-bound inquiry into the police action, stating that several students and members of the legal fraternity were injured

title: Sikkim NHPC tunnel disaster: Search for Malayali geologist continues
content: The tunnel under construction collapsed following an explosion in the ‘Head Race Tunnel’ and a geologist from Kottayam is feared trapped inside the tunnel after a landslide blocked its entrance

title: Student bodies to hold ‘Chalo Lok Bhavan’ protest on July 23
content: They seek Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak and call for support to the July 24 educational institutions bandh in Andhra Pradesh

title: Chipko movement was a challenge to hierarchy of development: Murali Sivaramakrishnan
content: Discussion held on the book 50 Years of Chipko – the Movement and its Afterlives  that has been edited by him

title: NEET: Congress, BJP workers detained during rival protests in Bengaluru
content: R. Ashok has alleged attack by Congress cadre and submitted a petition to the Bengaluru City Police Commissioner

title: Centre steps up outreach to Wangchuk, agitators; activist puts a rider to call off protest
content: Will call off hunger strike if govt. promises no action against students, says Wangchuk; “we are in talks with CJP and will continue to engage,” says Nadda, main interlocutor

title: ACB books energy utility divisional engineer in disproportionate assets case, seizes properties worth ₹27.65 crore
content: Ramana had earlier been trapped by ACB in December 2019 while serving as Divisional Engineer (Technical) in the Cyber City Circle of the Hyderabad electricity department.

title: Opposition, government spar over NEET debate in Rajya Sabha; House adjourned repeatedly
content: The government has been saying since the beginning of the session... all important issues, including NEET paper leak, will be discussed, said Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiran Rijiju

title: Siya Goyal, Lover Wanted To Injure Husband, But Later Dropped 'Risky' Plan
content: Siya Goyal, 20, and her 22-year-old lover, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly pushed her 26-year-old fiance, Ketan Agarwal, into a gorge from the Lohagad Fort near Pune on June 18.

title: End-Of-Season Sale Is Here. But Big Discounts Can Cost You More Money
content: Many brands have shortened sale periods, making genuine "80 per cent off everything" offers less common than shoppers may expect.

title: Iran-US War News Highlights: US Strikes Iran For 12th Consecutive Night
content: US-Iran War News Highlights: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: 4 Flats, 2 Kg Gold: Telangana Engineer Found With Assets Worth Rs 150 Crore
content: Officials detected bank deposits of Rs 52.02 lakh, 2.094 kg of gold ornaments worth about Rs 4 crore, three vehicles (Kia, Mahindra and Maruti Baleno) worth around Rs 45 lakh

title: Iran-US War Live: Houthis Attack Saudi Tankers In Red Sea As US Hits Iran Again
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in Hormuz, drawing an "eye for an eye" warning from Tehran as both sides sharpened threats in the escalating war.

title: Congress, CJP Protest Highlights: "I Want To Live, But Not At Cost of Youth," Says Sonam Wangchuk
content: Congress, CJP Protest Highlights: The huge protest over the NEET paper leak are continuing for the third straight day today.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Rajiv Chowk, ITO Among 16 Delhi Metro Stations Shut
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: Uttarakhand Nurse, Seeking A Job, Consumes Phenyl Outside Minister's House
content: An unemployed female nurse, seeking a job in the health department, allegedly consumed phenyl outside the residence of state health minister Subodh Uniyal in Dehradun.

title: 'No One Will Sell Oil If Tehran Can't': Iran's Big Warning To US, Middle East
content: The remarks came after US President Donald Trump warned that American forces will destroy one bridge or power plant each time Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Delhi-Dehradun Expressway Had 2 Potholes, Contractor Repaired Them: Centre
content: Responding to a question by Rajya Sabha MP Ashok Singh, Union Road Transport and Highways Minister Nitin Gadkari said the Delhi-Dehradun access-controlled highway is covered by a 10-year defect liability period.

title: Delhi's First Integrated Metro-Flyover To Be Ready By December
content: Commuters travelling through some of northeast Delhi's most congested roads could get major relief once the city's first integrated double-decker Metro-flyover project becomes operational.

title: DDA Housing Scheme 2026: 25% Discount On 1,200 Narela Flats. Check Prices
content: The Delhi Development Authority has announced a new housing scheme offering a 25 per cent discount on more than 1,200 ready-to-move flats in Narela.

title: At UN, India Slams Pakistan Over Terrorism, Kashmir, Indus Water Treaty
content: India on Wednesday slammed Pakistan for deploying cross-border terrorism as a "tool of state policy" and reiterated that Kashmir will always remain an integral and inalienable part of India.

title: US Announces Landmark Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia
content: The United States on Wednesday announced that it had reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal with Saudi Arabia.

title: Why New York's Zohran Mamdani Cannot Arrest Netanyahu. What The Law Says
content: The International Criminal Court, based in The Hague, issued an arrest warrant for Netanyahu in 2024 for alleged war crimes during the Gaza war. Israel rejects the court's jurisdiction and denies committing war crimes.

title: US Says It's Carrying Out 12th Night Of Strikes On Iran
content: The US military said Wednesday it had launched a fresh wave of strikes targeting Iranian military infrastructure, marking the twelfth night in a row it had attacked Iran.

title: US House Approves $95 Billion Budget Plan With Iran War Funding
content: The US House of Representatives approved a $95 billion budget framework on Wednesday, with most of the funding earmarked for the Pentagon to fund the war against Iran.

title: Assam Floods: 10 More Deaths In 24 Hours; Over 6 Lakh People Affected
content: At least 10 people, including four children, lost their lives due to floods in the last 24 hours in the state, according to the Assam State Disaster Management Authority.

title: Wildfires sweep Sicily as Europe battles extreme heat and soaring fire risk

title: Kalyan Banerjee suspended from Lok Sabha as rebel MPs complain of foul language
content: banerjee, not new to allegations of inappropriate behaviour, was suspended for the remainder of parliament's monsoon session . some women MPs of the party's breakaway faction accused him of using indecent language against them and complained to Speaker Om Birla . the motion was passed amid noisy protests by opposition members on the paper leak issue .

title: Complaint filed in NHRC over police ‘brutality’ on protesters at Jantar Mantar
content: a complaint has been filed with NHRC seeking independent inquiry into the alleged police “brutality” on July 20 against students protesting over the NEET-UG exam paper leak issue . the complaint includes allegations of misconduct by male police personnel against female students . congress member and advocate Virender Vashist in the complaint also urged the commission to look into allegations protesting students were subject to “excessive and disproportionate use of force”

title: PM Modi urges youth to lead vision of Viksit Bharat
content: PM Narendra Modi suggested that efforts be made to develop an "institutional mechanism" to break departmental silos and foster greater inter-departmental coordination . he also pitched for establishing India as a "global sports hub" by hosting major international sporting events .

title: 'Toughest thing I do': Donald Trump honours four US soldiers killed in Middle East

title: Parliament washout again; govt reaches out to opposition to end impasse
content: opposition and govt are locked in intense discussions to break the deadlock in parliament . both houses witness a washout of proceedings for the third consecutive day . a final view will be taken at a meeting of parties in Kharge's chamber .

title: Balen ends envoy snub amid protests, to meet India & China ambassadors
content: the two meetings are expected to be held on the same day, an arrangement meant to signal that both India and China remain central to Nepal’s foreign policy . he avoided individual meetings with envoys and visiting officials who would ordinarily expect one-on-one access to the head of govt .

title: Quad seeks free & open Indo-Pacific, pushes back against Chinese assertiveness
content: india's policy of "multi-alignment" and strategic autonomy evident in Manila . external affairs minister S Jaishankar met his china counterpart Wang Yi in the Philippines . the Quad meeting saw discussions on the situation in East China Sea and South China Sea .

title: ‘My mother doesn’t know I am injured’: Protesters stand firm at Jantar Mantar
content: Koushiki Saha, a trainee journalist currently reporting for The Times of India, covers urban governance, infrastructure lapses, public grievances, and municipal policies with clarity and compassion. Still learning every day, she draws insights from fieldwork, lived experiences, and holding authorities accountable through persistent, people-focused reporting. Read More

content: Aabhaas Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: Pharma companies in a flux over Trump's 200% tariff move on generics
content: A business journalist with around two decades of experience tracking key consumer-focussed sectors like consumer durables, retail, consumer goods, aviation, automobiles and advertising, as well as economic ministries of the Union government. Now, writes primarily on pharmaceuticals and healthcare, and on issues of consumer interest. Besides also looks at trends that are shaping consumer behaviour and the broad consumer landscape. \nYou can follow Rupali on Twitter@Rupalijee. title: NTA runs on 24 permanent staff, 197 contractual and outsourced hands
content: the agency handled over 6.6 crore candidate registrations since 2018 for competitive exams such as NEET-UG, JEE and UGC-NET . the agency has 39 permanent posts filled through deputation . but the post-wise strength disclosed by the education ministry adds up to 24 .

title: Assam floods worsen: 50 dead so far, over 100 reported missing
content: Prabin Kalita is a journalist at The Times of India and is currently the Chief of Bureau (northeast). He has been reporting in mainstream Indian national media since 2001. He has been a field journalist reporting gamut of issues from India’s northeastern region and major developments in neighbouring countries like Myanmar, China, Bhutan and Bangladesh concerning India and northeastern region. He has been covering insurgency—internal and cross-border, politics, natural calamities, environment etc. He is a post-graduate in Geological Sciences from Gauhati University. Read More

title: Karachi-bound bus attacked in Balochistan; 3 killed, 5 injured as curfew imposed
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: 'Bail by HCs shouldn't be challenged': SC rejects appeal by Chhattisgarh govt
content: the supreme court said that once a high court grants bail to an accused, it must be treated as final and SC must not entertain appeals against the order by the state . a bench of CJI Surya Kant, and justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana made the statement as they disposed of the appeal against bail grant to Chaitanya Baghel .
